description: Generate inclined-plane roll/slide OFAT matrices from initial state and friction parameters, solve them with MuJoCo, and replay one solver timeline through UE multi-view RGB/depth/instance segmentation.
physical_assumptions: The harness coordinate system is z-up and the default ramp downhill axis is +x.
The subject starts at rest and subsequent translation and rotation come from the solver, never from authored per-frame trajectories.
Under one locked slope, mass, map, asset, camera plan and timebase, increasing friction should reduce slip ratio and downhill displacement.
The runout may use a separate high friction value so every condition settles before the post-event tail ends.
required_assets: real visual mesh or declared analytic visual fallback
UE map and stable visual materials
verifier_rules: Trajectory source must be mujoco_ramp_friction and every frame must retain solver provenance.
The subject must contact the ramp, move downhill and lose height without unexplained pre-motion.
Low, medium and high friction conditions must respectively satisfy declared sliding, partial-roll and rolling slip-ratio bands.
Downhill displacement and final speed must fall inside each CaseSpec range.
All five cameras must share one 145-frame timeline with canonical RGB, metric depth EXR and stable instance segmentation EXR.
A visual material override on a multi-slot UE asset must cover every material slot; the analytic collider remains hidden from RGB.
repair_suggestions: Fix the inclined support relation using the surface normal instead of horizontal top-face snapping.
Keep collision geometry analytic and bind the real mesh only as a no-collision visual proxy.
When an asset appears in segmentation but not RGB, validate all material slots before changing physics or camera state.
Use the smoke to candidate cost funnel and stop before full sensor export if RGB observability fails.
Do not rank different friction conditions as quality winners; report them as a causal matrix.
